REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 9, 2023. THE COURT IS INCLINED TO GRANT THE MOTION FOR LEAVE TO ADD NEW CAUSES OF ACTION IF THE TRIAL IS CONTINUED. PLAINTIFF SHALL FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF (NOT TO EXCEED 3 PAGES) ADDRESSING WHETHER CITISCAPE MAY BE ADDED AS A NEW DEFENDANT (NOT A DOE DEFENDANT), I.E. TO ADDRESS THE STATUE OF LIMITATIONS NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 28, 2022. DEFENDANT MAY FILE A RESPONSE TO THE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF (NOT TO EXCEED 3 PAGES) NO LATER THAN JANUARY 3, 2023. BRIEFS SHALL BE LIMITED TO THE ISSUES EXPRESSLY AUTHORIZED BY THIS RULING. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-21-2022 Continued To Jan-09-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Douglas N. Akay; Akay Law; 415-764-1999; appearing for plaintiff Patricia Davis.
Terrence C. Heaslett; Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP; 925-357-3456; appearing for defendant 999 Summit Owners Association.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to January 9, 2023. The Court is inclined to grant the motion for leave to add new causes of action if the trial is continued. Plaintiff shall file a supplemental brief (not to exceed 3 pages) addressing whether CitiScape may be added as a new defendant (not a DOE defendant), i.e. to address the Statue of Limitations no later than December 28, 2022. Defendant may file a response to the supplemental brief (not to exceed 3 pages) no later than January 3, 2023. Briefs shall be limited to the issues expressly authorized by this ruling.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
